caveat: This framework content pack is a structured starter representation and does not constitute legal advice or complete regulatory coverage. This is a starter content pack for scoping purposes only. It does not constitute legal advice or a complete compliance determination.

obligations.title: Install and maintain network security controls
obligations.description: Install and maintain network security controls. Organizations should implement and maintain controls that satisfy this requirement.
obligations.applicability_notes: Applies to all system components in or connected to the cardholder data environment (CDE).

obligations.title: Processes and mechanisms for protecting stored account data are defined and understood
obligations.description: Processes and mechanisms for protecting stored account data are defined and understood. Organizations should implement and maintain controls that satisfy this requirement.
obligations.applicability_notes: Applies wherever account data is stored, including backups.

obligations.title: PAN is rendered unreadable wherever it is stored
obligations.description: PAN is rendered unreadable wherever it is stored using one-way hashes of the entire PAN, truncation, index tokens, or strong cryptography together with associated key-management processes.
obligations.applicability_notes: One-way hashing alone does not satisfy this requirement without truncation or additional controls per PCI SSC guidance.

obligations.title: Bespoke and custom software are developed securely
obligations.description: Bespoke and custom software are developed securely. Organizations should implement and maintain controls that satisfy this requirement.
obligations.applicability_notes: Includes secure coding training and code review prior to release.

obligations.title: Public-facing web applications are protected by an automated technical solution
obligations.description: Public-facing web applications are protected by an automated technical solution, such as a web-application firewall, that continually detects and prevents web-based attacks.
obligations.applicability_notes: Manual application review alone no longer satisfies this requirement under PCI DSS v4.0.

obligations.title: User authentication is established via at least one authentication method
obligations.description: User authentication is established via at least one authentication method. Organizations should implement and maintain controls that satisfy this requirement.
obligations.applicability_notes: Covers something-you-know, something-you-have, and something-you-are factors.

obligations.title: Multi-factor authentication (MFA) is implemented to secure access into the CDE
obligations.description: Multi-factor authentication (MFA) is implemented to secure access into the CDE. Organizations should implement and maintain controls that satisfy this requirement.
obligations.applicability_notes: Required for all access into the CDE, including administrator and remote access, under PCI DSS v4.0.

obligations.title: Audit logs are reviewed at least once daily using automated tools
obligations.description: Audit logs for all system components are reviewed at least once daily using automated log-review or security-event-alerting tools to identify anomalies or suspicious activity.
obligations.applicability_notes: Manual daily review of all logs no longer satisfies this requirement at scale under PCI DSS v4.0 guidance.

obligations.title: A penetration testing methodology covering application and network layers is defined
obligations.description: A penetration testing methodology is defined, documented, and implemented, covering application-layer and network-layer testing, consideration of threats and vulnerabilities experienced in the preceding 12 months, and a documented risk-based remediation approach.
obligations.applicability_notes: Testing must be performed at least annually and after significant infrastructure or application changes.

obligations.title: An incident response plan defines roles, communications, and containment procedures
obligations.description: An incident response plan is created, documented, and implemented, defining roles, responsibilities, communication strategies, and containment and mitigation procedures for suspected or confirmed security incidents.
obligations.applicability_notes: Plan must be tested at least annually and cover notification of payment brands and acquirers.

content_versions.obligation_text: Install and maintain network security controls covering all connections into and out of the cardholder data environment.
Render PAN unreadable wherever stored via one-way hash, truncation, index token, or strong cryptography with key management.
Implement multi-factor authentication for all access into the cardholder data environment.
Define and implement a documented penetration testing methodology covering application and network layers.
Create and maintain an incident response plan with defined roles and containment procedures.

control_suggestions.control_description: Require multi-factor authentication for all access into the cardholder data environment, including remote and administrative access.
Maintain and test a documented incident response plan covering roles, communications, and containment for security incidents affecting the CDE.
